name: n8n Workflow Builder MCP
what_it_does: An MCP server that connects AI assistants directly to a live n8n instance for full workflow lifecycle management — create, read, update, delete, activate/deactivate, execute, and monitor workflows and their executions via 15 MCP tools.
use_cases: Managing n8n workflows (CRUD) via natural language commands from Claude or Cline
Monitoring workflow execution history with filtering and pagination from an AI assistant
Triggering manual workflow executions and tracking their status
Organizing workflows with tags from within an AI development environment
Generating security audit reports for n8n instance configurations
not_for: Building new workflows from scratch using node documentation (use n8n-mcp for that)
Read-only workflow monitoring (over-featured for simple monitoring use cases)
best_when: When you want an AI assistant to act as a full operator of your n8n instance — managing, executing, and monitoring workflows without switching to the n8n UI.
avoid_when: When you need AI assistance building new complex workflows from node documentation; n8n-mcp provides better node coverage for authoring.

auth.notes: Requires N8N_HOST (instance URL) and N8N_API_KEY environment variables. API key generated from n8n Settings → API Keys.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed, free. Also available via Smithery.ai hosted deployment (separate terms).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No specific error documentation found; relies on n8n API error propagation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Workflow CRUD operations are standard REST semantics; idempotency not explicitly documented.
known_agent_gotchas: Operates directly on a live n8n instance — no sandbox mode, mistakes affect real workflows
Activate/deactivate tools can immediately affect running production automations
Security audit tool generates reports but does not remediate issues automatically
Smithery.ai hosted option may have different availability and data handling than self-hosted

security_score_components.security_notes: n8n workflow automation MCP. API key auth. Workflow credentials stored in n8n — protect admin access. Self-hostable for full control. Webhook endpoints are publicly accessible.
